## [00:00 - 03:02]

All right, so I'm going to give you a little bit of pre-framing before we get into the three testing frameworks. The main thing is there's going to be three things that any brand owner can do to test whether is it a change on Facebook or is it a change in my store or is it a change in my offering. Those are the most that we need to be implementing and it could be one, two or all three depending on what you need. The first, in any e-commerce brand you're going to want to look at what is that one thing that will drive the most impact with the least amount of effort. Let it sink in, right? That's where the first thing people usually try to do is change creative or change the angle. That's Facebook specific, right? Now after you do that, what's next? It's not working. I need it to work more. Those are the two questions we always constantly bounce between. It's either working or it's not working. If it's working, do more of it. If it's not working, stop doing that, right? Glasses, offer rotation or creation. Oftentimes a product would just put itself out there. For instance, like the glasses. Well, what if this glass wasn't selling? But as soon as you put the glasses with the case, that's a new offering. That's a new value add and to a consumer, the psychology of them buying one thing and getting another is a gift without devaluing the brand value. So let me paint this picture for you. This is a real conversation that I had with my media buyer that's in charge of multiple brands. Nick, Miracle Brand is seeing an overall performance drop for the past three weeks. We've just spent about $10,000. Whoa, Jake, what are you talking about? You just spent $10,000. What do you do? This is a major issue. How did you get through this? What did the first thing you wanted to do? We responded, we were like, well, we refreshed the creative. We took two of our most mentioned value propositions and we went through all of our Amazon reviews. Then what? Well, it wasn't good enough. It didn't work. It was like, okay, no problem. You do what you need to do. The next thing is we went to the site. I tested the checkout. I went all the way through the funnel, put the credit card in and you just bought yourself more sheets. Okay. Well, did it work? No, nothing's wrong there. I think the UX is fine. Lastly is, well, I know that our product is great. I've seen all the reviews and people really believe in it, but they're just not buying. I know if they try it, they're going to love it. So what did we do? We created a free trial. We wanted them to try before they buy it. As you scroll, it's pretty straightforward. They understand the experience. I know what I want to get. I want to try it before I buy it. This is the easiest way for us to troubleshoot where we need to put our attention. Is it on the creative? If it's not, is it on the website optimization? And if it's not, is it on the offer, rotation or creation? Those are the three things. So if you were to take any inventory, this is the final step before we actually get into

building the campaigns. Where does my focus go? Where can I make the most impact with the least amount of effort? Look at your store. If something's not working, let's go through the checklist.
